channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over migrant deportations, and appears to take direct aim at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration owes US business millions in unpaid bills amid USAID shutdown, lawsuit says
item.description: A new lawsuit accuses the Trump administration of stiffing U.S. businesses of hundreds of millions of dollars in unpaid bills for work that has already been done amid its dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli cabinet backs Trump’s demand for Hamas to release all hostages by Saturday deadline
item.description: Israel’s security cabinet supports President Trump’s demand that Hamas release all of its hostages by noon on Saturday, an Israeli official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance, Bessent to meet with Zelensky amid Trump's push to end the war in Ukraine
item.description: Vice President Vance and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent are set to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky amid the administration’s push for the war with Russia to end.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky proposes swap of seized territory with Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is proposing an exchange of seized territory with Russia as part of any potential negotiations to end the war. Ukraine holds control of a small pocket of Russian territory, parts of Kursk Oblast, captured during a surprise incursion in August.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: La Scala exhibition celebrates the theater's ballet corps with candid behind-the-scenes photos
item.description: Photographer Gérard Uféras has been granted full access to the La Scala ballet corps’ backstage over six years to produce a series of emotional and candid portraits at the heart of a new exhibition at the theater’s museum and a soon-to-be published book.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dozens of US organizations condemn Trump Gaza plan
item.description: Social justice organizations based in the U.S. issued a joint statement Monday condemning President Trump’s plans to redevelop the Gaza Strip, effectively displacing Palestinians from their homeland.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers traveling abroad are targeted for alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An Israeli army reservist's dream vacation in Brazil ended abruptly last month over an accusation that he committed war crimes in the Gaza Strip. Yuval Vagdani woke up on Jan.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump pauses enforcement of foreign bribery ban
item.description: President Trump has signed an executive order to direct the Department of Justice to stop enforcing a law that prevents bribery toward foreign government officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel’s fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman puts the focus on West Bank violence
item.description: KAFR AL-LABAD, West Bank (AP) — The call came in the middle of the night, Mohammed Shula said. His daughter-in-law, eight months pregnant with her first child, was whispering. There was panic in her voice. “Help, please,” Shula recalled her saying. “You have to save us.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine and Russia trade long-range attacks as officials probe possibilities for peace
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces targeted Ukraine’s energy and gas infrastructure in a “massive” nighttime missile attack and Ukrainian drones struck an important oil refinery inside Russia, officials said Tuesday, as the near three-year war rumbled on against a backdrop of renewed diplomatic eff
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Corruption survey gives many nations worst scores in over a decade. South Sudan slides to the bottom
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Many countries had their worst showing in more than a decade in an index released Tuesday that serves as a barometer of public sector corruption worldwide, from leading powers such as the United States and France to authoritarian nations such as Russia and Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN suspends its humanitarian work in Houthi rebels' stronghold in Yemen after more staff detentions
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it suspended its humanitarian operations in the stronghold of Yemen’s Houthi rebels after they detained eight more U.N. staffers, affecting the global response to one of the world’s worst humanitarian disasters. In a statement, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump steps up his 2018 tariffs on steel and aluminum, risking inflation on promise of more jobs
item.description: President Donald Trump has removed the exceptions and exemptions from his 2018 tariffs on steel, meaning that all steel imports will be taxed at a minimum of 25%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's delaying next hostage release, claiming ceasefire violations
item.description: Hamas is delaying the next release of Oct. 7 hostages, accusing Israel of violating the terms of their ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With Trump in the White House, Iranians mark the anniversary of the 1979 Islamic Revolution
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Tens of thousands of Iranians marked the anniversary of the country's 1979 Islamic Revolution, the first such rally since President Donald Trump returned to the White House and restarted his “maximum pressure” campaign targeting Tehran.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday. The halo, known as an Einstein ring, encircles a galaxy 590 million light-years away, considered close by cosmic standards.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer advance to presidential runoff
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump remains committed to US owning Gaza, says Middle East states could help rebuild war-torn area
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is committed to the U.S. purchasing and owning Gaza, but that he may allow Middle Eastern countries to rebuild sections of the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US officials are bound for Europe for top-level talks on Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump’s national security adviser said Sunday that top administration officials will meet with European officials this week about how to end the war in Ukraine, nearly three years after Russia launched an all-out invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sam Nujoma, Namibia's fiery freedom fighter and first president, dies aged 95
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Sam Nujoma, the fiery, white-bearded freedom fighter who led Namibia to independence from apartheid South Africa in 1990 and served as its first president for 15 years, coming to be known as the father of his nation, has died. He was 95.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, FARAI MUTSAKA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Days before Trump halted funding, an ex-Israeli hostage was held at UNRWA school in Gaza, she reveals
item.description: Former Israeli hostage Emily Damari said she was held in an UNRWA center in Gaza. Damari was recently released as part of the hostages for ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage released 491 days after he was kidnapped and his family was slaughtered
item.description: Eli Sharabi, who was released after spending 491 days in Hamas captivity, will return home without his wife or two daughters, who were killed on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One of the victims of Sweden's deadliest mass shooting called his fiancée to say he loved her
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Wounded and bleeding, Salim Karim Iskef managed to video-call his fiancée to tell her he loved her one last time before he died this week in what was Sweden's deadliest mass shooting, The 28-year-old asked Kareen Alia to look after his mother and herself before the call ended.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation
